title: Implement MeshCore app message ingress into LICHEN local interface
description: Why this exists: app compatibility should allow MeshCore clients to inject local messages into LICHEN without changing RF protocol. What needs to be done: decode MeshCore client send-message operations, validate destination/group/channel semantics for the supported subset, translate to LICHEN local message/CoAP or internal send API, and return compatible acknowledgements/errors. Acceptance criteria: tests send a MeshCore app message and observe the expected LICHEN local message call; unsupported destinations/groups return explicit errors; payload length/encoding limits are enforced; no MeshCore RF frame is transmitted over LoRa.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.2 contract: channel/direct text ingress should target a shared app-interface event/send contract, not MeshCore RF and not a one-off gateway-local path. project-LICHEN-gbx6 tracks creating that shared Zephyr app-interface boundary. Until then, CMD_SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG and direct SEND_TXT_MSG should return deterministic unsupported/not-found rather than pretending to send over MeshCore RF.
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.14 identity projection: direct SEND_TXT_MSG can succeed only when the 6-byte MeshCore prefix maps to exactly one known LICHEN peer Ed25519 public key. Zero matches return NOT_FOUND; prefix collisions return BAD_STATE/ILLEGAL_ARG; never choose a peer silently. Channel text is unaffected by peer-prefix lookup but still must not imply MeshCore RF.
